page: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 64 shared vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in memory bandwidth and data processing. The server achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3710.38 and a multi-core score of 118152.34. Memory read-only bandwidth reached 7142.64 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 532.73 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 550.47 GB/sec, while Passmark database operations scored 42060.8, reflecting strong data-handling efficiency.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable compute performance, though the high core count and massive memory capacity mitigate resource constraints for memory-heavy applications. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive tasks that do not require dedicated CPU cores or large local storage. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, Redis deployments, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs (32 physical cores) clocked at 2.0 GHz and 512.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes a 1 GB network storage volume and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, with large block read speeds of 532.73 GB/sec and write speeds of 251.91 GB/sec. The instance excels in data processing, scoring 42060.8 on Passmark database operations and processing 111.5 million Redis SET operations per second. While the shared CPU allocation is a tradeoff for sustained compute-heavy tasks, the high memory density makes this server highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is optimized for large databases, in-memory caching, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized server with 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four vCPUs and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier memory read bandwidth of up to 7142.64 GB/sec
1 GB network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address
Strong performance in database operations and Redis caching
Suitable for memory-intensive workloads and medium-scale LLM inference
